expectations.text: Explicitly names the SUCCESs principles (at least 4 of the 6: Simple, Unexpected, Concrete, Credible, Emotional, Stories)
expectations.evidence: The output contains only a tagline and 7 alternatives with no commentary. SUCCESs principles are never mentioned by name.

expectations.text: References or applies the Curse of Knowledge concept
expectations.evidence: No mention of Curse of Knowledge or any related concept anywhere in the 81-word output.

expectations.text: Pushes concrete sensory language over abstract jargon
expectations.evidence: The output provides no commentary, analysis, or rationale pushing concrete over abstract. It is a bare list of tagline options with no discussion.

expectations.text: The proposed tagline is concrete and sensory, not abstract
expectations.evidence: Lead tagline 'Stop sounding like your industry. Start sounding like yourself.' uses the concrete sensory verb 'sounding' and a picturable referent ('your industry').

expectations.text: Includes an unexpected twist, reversal, or counterintuitive element (the U in SUCCESs)
expectations.evidence: The Stop/Start structure is a reversal pattern. Alternatives also contain reversals: 'Unlearn the industry accent' and 'Leaders don't need a bigger voice. They need their own.'
